raw_cv_text:
Fiona Frost
l | github.com/Muhammed-Nawfal |Birmingham, UK
EDUCATION
University of Birmingham Birmingham, UK
BSc Computer Science Graduating June 2026
Grade: Predicted First-class (1:1)
Relevant modules – Software Engineering and Professional Practice, Agile Team Project, Full Stack Application Development,
Operating Systems and Systems Programming, Data Structures and Algorithms, Artificial Intelligence.
Chettinad Vidyashram Chennai, India
Class 12 Senior School Certificate (CBSE - Science Stream) May 2023
Grade: 95.2% (A*A*A*)
WORK EXPERIENCE
LyncFit Remote (UK)
Software Engineering Intern (Part-time) June 2025 - Present
• Built core front-end features of the React (JavaScript) based web app for run-clubs to manage events and members efficiently.
• Designed reusable UI components for chat, forum, and broadcast pages, improving consistency and reducing duplicate code.
• Developed ~25% of the mobile app’s front-end using Flutter (Dart), including Rewards and Profile sections, for general users.
• Integrated frontend with Spring Boot APIs and managed state, background data refreshing and light caching using services
and Provider package, ensuring the UI reflects live backend updates.
• Collaborated with a cross-functional team in an agile environment, contributing to decision-making processes on key UI
changes and fixing bugs to enhance user experience.
Marks & Spencer Food Birmingham, UK
Christmas Customer Assistant December 2024
• Replenished 200+ products per shift overnight, ensuring stock availability for morning opening to improve sales.
• Worked with a team of 15+ to check expiries for product freshness, demonstrating reliability and efficiency under pressure.
PROJECTS
AI Gym Coach – Personal Project August 2025 - Present
• Planned and set up a full-stack fitness app to help gym users log workouts, track goals, and access AI-powered gym Q&A, to
address early dropouts from gym due to lack of proper guidance.
• Defined requirements and architecture, initiating backend development with Spring Boot (Java) and PostgreSQL, and
integrating a React (TypeScript) frontend with an OpenAI-powered Node.js microservice for workout generation and Q&A.
Agile Team Project – University of Birmingham January 2025 – May 2025
• Developed the user matching feature of a full-stack web student networking app, implementing accept/decline profile flows
and match request handling while collaborating with a team of eight for other features.
• Built the front end in Angular (TypeScript) with HTML/CSS and integrated it with a Spring Boot (Java) backend and PostgreSQL
database, within a JHipster (JDL) application, following Agile methodology and version control with GitHub.
• Coordinated project planning and guided less technical teammates to ensure smooth project submission.
TECHNICAL SKILLS
Programming Languages & Databases: Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, Dart, HTML, SQL (PostgreSQL), C.
Frameworks & Tools: React, Flutter, Spring Boot, Angular, JHipster, REST APIs, Git.
Operating Systems: Microsoft Windows, Linux.
ACHIEVEMENTS
Encode Club - AI Hackathon April 2024
• Built a React-based “internet of mini-apps” focused on collaborative group studying, in 48 hours – NoteBank (note-sharing),
SemSync (deadline tracking), and CramBot (flashcard generation with Ollama AI API).
• Developed the full UI for NoteBank as part of a team of four, and integrated all three apps over a shared backend and
database using the Tonk stack. The project was awarded first place in the bounty category.
Transport for West Midlands - The Birmingham Project Birmingham, UK
The Birmingham Project June 2024
• Designed a plan for HS2’s Curzon Street station, focusing on accessible transport solutions for disabled people.
• Collaborated with a team to develop solutions through analysis of disability, transport, engineering and cost data.
• Created a PowerPoint video independently to present the team’s solutions, earning first place among five teams.
POSITION OF RESPONSIBILITY
Event Success Team Volunteer London, UK
PropTech Connect 2025 September 2025
• Supported 6000+ attendees at Europe’s largest real estate tech event, assisting with queries, navigation and the networking
app at the Help Desk while developing professionalism, communication, and problem-solving skills.
• Engaged with exhibitors on emerging tech solutions, strengthening understanding of technology integration in real estate.
